title: streptanthus glandulosus subsp. sonomensis
intro: Streptanthus glandulosus subsp. sonomensis, also known as Sonoma jewelflower, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the coastal mountains of California, USA. It belongs to the mustard family (Brassicaceae) and is characterized by its bright yellow and purple flowers. The medicinal properties of Sonoma jewelflower include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as skin irritations and infections. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and are used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
